description: This figure displays microstructures resulting from cellular transformation and spinodal decomposition in four Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels after tempering at 800 °C. The upper row of each subfigure is optical microscopy (OM) micrograph for low-magnification overview, and the lower row is scanning electron microscopy (SEM) secondary electron image for high-resolution microstructural characterization. Subfigures (a) to (d) correspond to 9Al-10Mn-1C, 12Al-10Mn-1C, 12Al-20Mn-1C, and 12Al-30Mn-1C alloys respectively. 9Al-10Mn-1C shows mixed κ+α+γ lamellar structures from cellular transformation and partial spinodal decomposed regions; 12Al-10Mn-1C presents fully cellular transformation-derived κ-carbide lamellae; 12Al-20Mn-1C features early-stage cellular transformation at grain boundaries plus partial spinodal decomposition; 12Al-30Mn-1C exhibits fully spinodal decomposed austenite with nanoscale κ-carbide precipitates. The figure verifies that increasing Mn content shifts the dominant austenite transformation from cellular transformation to spinodal decomposition, and both transformations contribute to hardness enhancement of the steels.
